title: ROYAL CARIBBEAN LIGHTS UP ICON OF THE SEAS WITH NEW BARS, NIGHTLIFE
text: The First-of-its-Kind Vacation Sets the Bar with More Hot Spots for Sips, Live Music and Ways to Play MIAMI , March 28, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Making every hour the happiest of hours on the highly anticipated Icon of the Seas , Royal Caribbean International has unveiled more than 15 of the bars and nightlife experiences that will light up the world's best vacation when the sun sets. Parents, couples and friends can make a toast to vacations and celebrations at new and returning hot spots for every type of adventurer.

title: Buy Royal Caribbean Instead Of Carnival Corporation
text: Cruising industry is expected to see strong growth throughout 2023. While Carnival Cruise is expecting a loss in 2023, Royal Caribbean is expecting to return to profitability.

title: Celebrity Cruises' Next Edge-Series Ship Brings New Flex Fuel Engine to Industry
text: Royal Caribbean Group's new ship will be at the forefront of alternative fuel use thanks to partnership with technology group Wärtsilä and shipyard Chantiers de L'Atlantique MIAMI , March 28, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- When Celebrity Cruises introduces the fifth ship in the revolutionary Edge Series, it will feature the collaborative efforts of its parent company Royal Caribbean Group, technology group Wärtsilä and shipyard Chantiers de L'Atlantique (CdA) to advance the use of alternative fuels for the cruise industry. The ship continues the innovation that debuted with the Edge Series but will also be equipped with a new engine model, along with storage and delivery systems, that will give unmatched fuel flexibility, with the ability to use three types of fuel, including methanol.

title: Royal Caribbean (RCL) Stock Up 40% in 6 Months: More Room to Run?
text: Royal Caribbean (RCL) emphasizes on enhancing its e-commerce and pre-cruise capabilities and optimizing its distribution channels to drive growth.

title: Better Buy: Royal Caribbean or Norwegian Cruise Line Stock?
text: Royal Caribbean earned more than twice as much revenue in the fourth quarter than in the same period in 2021. Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings had 24% higher onboard revenue last quarter than in 2019.

title: Why Cruise Stocks Popped Before Carnival's Earnings
text: Carnival reports earnings on Monday next week. Most analysts predict a big loss, but Deutsche Bank is optimistic about Carnival stock in the short term.

title: You're Right: Stocks Are Much Riskier Now (But Still Worth It)
text: If the S&P 500's swings are putting you on edge, don't think it's just you. Stocks are measurably riskier than they used to be.

title: Royal Caribbean Group Appoints Rebecca Yeung to Board of Directors
text: MIAMI , March 20, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Today, Royal Caribbean Group (NYSE: RCL) announced the appointment of Rebecca Yeung, corporate vice president, operations science and advanced technology at FedEx Corporation (NYSE: FDX), to its Board of Directors, effective March 15, 2023. "Ms. Yeung brings a valuable combination of skills to our board, as she is well-versed in technology, digitization and business strategy," said Richard D.

title: South Africa Bakery Products Market Report 2023: Growing Demand for Affordable and Ready-To-Eat Products Boosts Sector - ResearchAndMarkets.com
text: DUBLIN--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The "South Africa Bakery Products Market | Trends, Value, Revenue, Outlook, Forecast, Size, Analysis, Growth, Industry, Share, Segmentation & COVID-19 IMPACT: Market Forecast By Product Types, By Distribution Channels And Competitive Landscape" report has been added to ResearchAndMarkets.com's offering. The South Africa Bakery Products Market grew moderately in 2018 and 2019, owing to a increase in consumption by the growing population. Furthermore, government's initiative 'South Africa's Township and Rural Entrepreneurship Programme (TREP),' in which the government gave $1 million to 1,400 owner-managed micro, home-based, co-operative bakeries and confectioneries, is accelerating the country's bakery product growth. According to the publisher, the South Africa Bakery Products Market revenue size is projected to grow at a CAGR of 2.7% from 2022-to 2028. The Bakery Products Market in south Africa will obtain growth over the forecast period since the demand for these products is growing in the country. South Africa Bakery Products Industry is an essential part of the Africa Bakery Products Market. Over the past few years, bakery products market in South Africa grew steadily on account of growing demand for affordable and ready-to-eat products among consumers due to fast-paced lifestyle and constrained spending among majority class of the country. Development of the retail sector, with expansion of supermarket/ hyper market networks across key cities along with development of retail store chains would continue to support growth of the domestic bakery industry. There are a number of key companies operating in this sector and with their hard work and efforts they have been adding value to the South Africa Bakery Products Market growth. Market by Product Types Bread, traditionally being a key ingredient of the staple diet in the country accounts for a major chunk of the South Africa bakery products market revenues. However, several factors such as prevalence of home baking, consumer preference for lower value product and increasing wheat prices are impeding growth of the market. Bread will remain the highest growing product type segment in the market. Market by Distribution Channels Convenience store held significant revenues in South Africa bakery product market on account of wide presence and proximity across diverse residential developments. This segment will grow more in the years to come. title: Royal Caribbean (RCL) Upgraded to Buy: Here's What You Should Know
text: Royal Caribbean (RCL) has been upgraded to a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), reflecting growing optimism about the company's earnings prospects. This might drive the stock higher in the near term.

title: Why Carnival, Norwegian, and Royal Caribbean Stocks Dropped Today
text: Don't look now, but another bank is in trouble: Credit Suisse. Investors are starting to worry that problems with banking could spread into the credit markets and make loans more expensive.

title: Why Carnival, Norwegian, and Royal Caribbean Stocks Dropped Today
text: Cruise line stocks are sinking Monday, two weeks after Norwegian Cruise reported an earnings miss and weak guidance. Investors may be wondering if Carnival Corporation will have similarly bad news when it reports earnings -- two weeks from now.

title: CALLING UP-AND-COMING ARTISTS, ROYAL CARIBBEAN LAUNCHES ART PROGRAM TO DEBUT ON ICON OF THE SEAS
text: First Up in the "Artist Discovery Program" Will be a Spotlight on Local Caribbean Artists MIAMI , March 10, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Royal Caribbean International has launched a search for iconic artistry to bring the Caribbean spirit to life on the iconic canvas that is the world's best family vacation, Icon of the Seas . In the new " Artist Discovery Program," up-and-coming artists from destinations the cruise line visits can vie to put their work on display for millions of vacationers who will set sail for years to come.

title: Better Cruise Stock Buy: Royal Caribbean or Lindblad Expeditions?
text: Demand for Royal Caribbean cruises remains high, and guests are spending more onboard. Lindblad Expeditions benefits from a blossoming adventure-travel market.

title: ROYAL CARIBBEAN'S BEACH CLUB IN THE BAHAMAS MOVES FORWARD FOR 2025 OPENING
text: The Royal Beach Club at Paradise Island Will Create a Unique Investment Opportunity for Bahamians MIAMI , March 8, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Royal Caribbean International, the world's largest and most popular cruise line, is moving ahead with the development of the first Royal Beach Club after receiving approval from the government of The Bahamas. The 17-acre destination experience coming to the western end of Paradise Island in Nassau, The Bahamas , will combine the island's striking beaches with the cruise line's signature experiences to create the ultimate beach day – The Royal Beach Club at Paradise Island.

title: After weathering pandemic storm, cruise-line stocks offer upside, says Stifel
text: Like the rest of the travel industry, cruise operators have weathered a pandemic-related storm over the last couple of years, but they now deserve attention from investors, says analyst firm Stifel.

title: AI Stock Headlines 5 Leaders Flashing Buy Signals As Market Rally Gains Steam
text: Arista Networks, which keeps popping up on lists of top AI stocks, leads this weekend's watch list of 5 stocks near buy points. The post AI Stock Headlines 5 Leaders Flashing Buy Signals As Market Rally Gains Steam appeared first on Investor's Business Daily.
